domain: Tongan ngatu bark cloth (Pacific tapa): 3-4 laminated layers with orthogonal grain orientation per layer; tapioca paste adhesive; predicts anisotropic strength as function of layer count + grain orientation; layer-count and orientation drive composite strength.
specific_mechanism: Multi-layer laminate with orthogonal grain per layer + tapioca paste adhesive; structural strength quantitatively predictable from layer count, grain orientation, paste strength.
llm_application: NGATU-LAMINATE quantitative scaling-law prediction: fit calibration curve P̂(L, θ, σ²) = α · L^β · sin(θ) · σ^γ relating LLM downstream performance P to (L = layer count, θ = average inter-layer init-orthogonality of weight matrices, σ² = residual-stream variance). Fit α, β, γ on small calibration set of trained models. Use to predict downstream performance BEFORE training given architecture parameters. Differs from (a) Chinchilla scaling (parameters + tokens not orthogonality), (b) μP parameterization invariance (not prediction), (c) Deep Language Networks (stacks not predicts), (d) PINN material property (physical not LLM) by combining (i) LAYER-COUNT + INIT-ORTHOGONALITY + RESIDUAL-VARIANCE features + (ii) QUANTITATIVE SCALING LAW + (iii) CALIBRATION FIT.
novelty_claim: Chinchilla scaling uses params+tokens. μP parameterization invariance. PINN material properties. NGATU-LAMINATE recombines (a) LAYER-COUNT + INIT-ORTHOGONALITY + RESIDUAL-VARIANCE + (b) SCALING LAW form + (c) CALIBRATION FIT. Three known components combined; init-orthogonality as predictor is the novelty axis.
